steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem to the rectangle
steps.content:
In a rectangle, the diagonal and two sides form a right triangle. Let $b$ be the longer side:
$$3^2 + b^2 = (3\sqrt{17})^2$$

steps.title: Solve for the longer side
steps.content:
Square the right side:
$$(3\sqrt{17})^2 = 9 \cdot 17 = 153$$
$$9 + b^2 = 153$$
$$b^2 = 144$$
$$b = 12$$
The longer side is 12.
